feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: The late-night eating habit that may throw your gut health into chaos
entry.summary: Eating late at night may compound the harmful impacts of stress on your digestive health and gut microbiome, according to recent research.

entry.title: HIV enters the brain and doesn’t leave – paradoxically, drugs intended to reduce brain inflammation increase virus levels
entry.summary: Immune cells can carry HIV into the brain, leading to problems with memory and cognition. Blocking other immune cells from the brain can make matters worse.

entry.title: As vaccination rates plunge in Pennsylvania schools, measles cases surge in largest outbreak in three decades
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Our Neighbors Asked to Speak With Us. Then They Showed Us Something We Can’t Ignore.
entry.summary: I don't think her solution is going to work out.

entry.title: Naked mole-rats age so slowly, resist cancer so well and survive oxygen loss so strangely that researchers now study them as one of nature’s best clues to human ageing.
entry.summary: The naked mole-rat is a small, hairless, more or less blind rodent that lives in colonies under the deserts of East Africa. It is about the size of a mouse, and a mouse lives around four years. The naked mole-rat lives more than thirty, with the oldest on rec…

entry.title: Why the U.S. Is Unprepared for a Potential Public Health Outbreak at the World Cup
entry.summary: The U.S. spent $625 million on World Cup security—none of it went to public health. Just days from the tournament, big concerns remain.

entry.title: Children who sleep poorly ‘turn into depressed teenagers’
entry.summary: Academics support consistent bedtimes, reduced screen time before bed, physical activity during the day and a calm sleep environment

entry.title: In a first, human receives multiple pig organs
entry.summary: A patient received two kidneys and a whole liver from a pig, the first example of multi-organ xenotransplantation.

entry.title: Everyone Warned Me About The Side Effects Of GLP-1s. Nobody Warned Me About The Judgment.
entry.summary: "I didn’t tell anyone I was starting a GLP-1 for this exact reason."

entry.title: Ebola outbreak live updates: WHO and Africa CDC unveil $518 million plan as DRC cases pass 450
entry.summary: A Kenyan court has blocked a proposed U.S. Ebola quarantine facility that has triggered protests in the country.

entry.title: The Probiotic Breakthrough for Natural Anxiety Relief and Better Mental Health
entry.summary: A surprising gut-brain discovery suggests that anxiety could one day be treated with specially designed probiotics. Could anxiety be shaped, at least in part, by tiny organisms living in the gut? Research from Duke-NUS Medical School and the National Neurosci…

entry.title: World Cup creates perfect conditions for infectious diseases to spread – here are the biggest threats health experts are watching for
entry.summary: Respiratory diseases like measles and flu pose a much bigger threat to public health for the World Cup than does Ebola.

entry.title: Cancer Death of Retired SF Firefighter Reignites Calls to Reform Blue Shield Coverage
entry.summary: The death of San Francisco firefighter Ken Jones has renewed calls to reform Blue Shield, the city's designated insurance provider. The late firefighter’s battle to treat his stage 4 lung cancer first gained attention in January, with advocates now blaming de…

entry.title: Popular weight loss drug may slow down biological aging: Study
entry.summary: The study found that the pace of biological aging for people who are living with HIV slowed by nine percent.

entry.title: Huge Psilocybin Dose Has Incredible Effect on Elderly Dementia Patient
entry.summary: A radical new treatment may be emerging.

entry.title: You Can Tell How Miserable Someone Is By 10 Things They Care About Way Too Much
entry.summary: Even if they don't verbalize it, you can tell how miserable someone is by 10 things they care about way too much. They're often stuck in their heads, and it becomes obvious to those around them.

entry.title: Your earlobes may hold a sneaky clue about future heart disease
entry.summary: While there are some clear signs of heart disease, like shortness of breath or chest pain, others aren’t as well-known, even when they’re in plain sight.

entry.title: 4 Gentle Exercises That Build Shoulder Strength Better Than Heavy Lifting After 60
entry.summary: A trainer shares 4 gentle shoulder exercises after 60 that rebuild strength with light resistance, no heavy lifting.

entry.title: This Simple Bedtime Tweak Cuts Your Risk of Major Cardiac Events in Half
entry.summary: Getting a solid eight hours of sleep per night sounds wonderful in theory. In reality? Between late-night TV, racing thoughts at 11 p.m. and the occasional g...

entry.title: Most people ignore Fatty Liver until it's too late: Liver transplant surgeon explains why weight loss could change everything
entry.summary: For years, fatty liver disease was often brushed aside as a minor finding on an ultrasound report. Many people never felt any symptoms. Others assumed it was a condition linked only to alcohol consumption. Today, doctors are seeing a very different picture.Ac…

entry.title: Woman Thought She Had a Stomach Bug, and Doctors Thought She Had an Autoimmune Disease. It Was Actually Something More Deadly
entry.summary: Audrey Leishman was placed in a medical coma due to her health issues

entry.title: The sleep industrial complex is lying to you — and it's making Americans sicker
entry.summary: Adults accept it as conventional health wisdom that we require eight hours of sleep each night in order to be healthy. Yet an expert on human evolutionary biology recently argued that this might actually be misleading.“Do you lose sleep over whether you sleep…

entry.title: Health workers at the epicenter of Congo’s Ebola outbreak labor with little pay or rest
entry.summary: Health workers inside the epicenter of Congo's outbreak say they are working with little pay or rest. The mining town of Mongbwalu attracts a huge number of laborers for large gold mines who live in crowded mining camps. That makes the transmission of the dis…

entry.title: The #1 Herb to Decrease Inflammation, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Plus, tips from a chef on how to use it in your kitchen.

entry.title: Pioneering Australian doctor Richard Scolyer dies after brain cancer battle
entry.summary: The melanoma doctor's brain cancer treatment experiment has sparked hope for a breakthrough.

entry.title: The promises and pitfalls of GLP-1 drugs
entry.summary: Approved 20 years ago as a diabetes treatment, GLP-1 drugs have been found to help patients reduce weight, changing the lives of more than 30 million people in the U.S. But there also have been troubling side effects reported.

entry.title: One UVM lab’s surprise discovery led to flu science breakthrough
entry.summary: Proteins in the two most common types of flu virus enable them to take very different paths to entering human lungs, a finding that could lead to more directed antiviral treatments.

entry.title: 7 Signs You're Aging Well, According To Geriatricians
entry.summary: Doctors reveal what they look for when examining a patient's longevity and how you can adopt the behaviors for yourself.

entry.title: This Daily Habit Might Be One Of The Easiest Ways To Improve Your Bone Density
entry.summary: When done correctly, this routine behavior can have noteworthy results, according to doctors.

entry.title: So THAT'S Why Your Stomach Hurts More As You Get Older
entry.summary: If the foods you love don’t seem to be loving you back anymore, you’re not imagining things. Here’s how to turn things around.

entry.title: Doctors May Be Overlooking the Real Cause of Persistent Arthritis Pain
entry.summary: A new study suggests that lingering rheumatoid arthritis symptoms are not always caused by ongoing inflammation.

entry.title: Struggle Sleeping? These 3 Sleep Habits Are Tied to Signs of Brain Aging, Study Finds
entry.summary: How we sleep may have lasting impacts for our brain health, with 3 common sleep behaviors linked to signs of brain aging and decline.

entry.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment
entry.summary: A traditional Chinese medicinal root used for over a thousand years is attracting new scientific attention for its potential to combat hair loss. Studies suggest Polygonum multiflorum can block harmful hormones, activate hair-growth signals, protect follicles…

entry.title: Gone without a trace
entry.summary: Winston Watson knows the communities between Negril, Westmoreland, and Montego Bay, St James, better than he ever wanted to.For more than two weeks, he and his relatives have criss-crossed western Jamaica chasing reported sightings of his missing father, Wins…

entry.title: Diabetes: Hypertension drugs may speed up kidney disease
entry.summary: Dihydropyridine calcium-channel-blockers (DCCBs), used to treat high blood pressure, could speed up the progression of kidney disease in people with type 2 diabetes, a new study claims.

entry.title: Measles emerges in California wastewater as health experts sound alarm
entry.summary: California health officials discovered evidence of measles in routine wastewater testing as the state faces a sharp resurgence of the disease.

entry.title: Rabid Fox Attacks Person, Dogs
entry.summary: The Rockville City Police Department is advising residents in and around the Potomac Woods and Horizon Hills neighborhoods to remain alert after a rabid fox reportedly attacked a man and […]

entry.title: Cardio or strength training: Harvard study finally picks one for longevity and reduced mortality risk
entry.summary: Wellness News: For decades, the fitness world has been locked in a never-ending debate: should you focus on cardio, like marathon running and brisk walking, or inves.

entry.title: Experts Say Constantly Worrying You're A Bad Person Could Be This Form Of OCD
entry.summary: "It's like our brains are almost stuck on a highway loop, and they can’t get off the highway."

entry.title: Researchers Discover a Hidden Vitamin D Problem That Persists Year-Round
entry.summary: A new study suggests that some groups may not experience the expected seasonal boost in vitamin D levels, even during the sunniest months of the year.

entry.title: South Africa commits $72 million in bid to shed its status as the country with the world's largest HIV epidemic
entry.summary: South Africa has committed R1

entry.title: Woman, 28, Says Debilitating Migraine Symptoms Are Fixed in Just 15 Minutes When She Eats This Nostalgic Candy
entry.summary: Jemma O'Donovan described the discovery as "a complete accident"

entry.title: The disease detectives suiting up for the World Cup
entry.summary: Public-health officials are trying to use a Covid-era playbook without pandemic-era funding.

entry.title: Local Health Officials Warn Of Hantavirus Dangers
entry.summary: As many continue to do their spring cleaning and clearing, they give advice on how to protect yourself from the deadly virus.

entry.title: Touch Neurons That Drive Chronic Eczema Itch Revealed
entry.summary: A new study discovers a dedicated mechanical itch pathway linked to peach fuzz hairs, opening new targets for eczema treatment.

entry.title: The 10 Unhealthiest Protein Bars On Store Shelves In 2026
entry.summary: Protein bars may be packed with protein, but they might also contain some less-than-savory ingredients. Here are the most unhealthy brands and bars in stores.

entry.title: One Drink of Alcohol a Day Raises Your Risk of 10 Cancers, Study Warns
entry.summary: People who enjoy a cheeky drink every now and then usually do so knowing that it's not particularly good for their health.

entry.title: Tracking the 2026 Ebola outbreak in maps and figures: Locations, severity, how contagious it is and more
entry.summary: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

entry.title: 5 Daily Exercises Men Should Do Each Morning to Build Leg Strength After 60
entry.summary: A CPT shares 5 daily morning moves that build leg strength and stability for men over 60.

entry.title: Poison center reports rose 39% for a common supplement — after it was incorrectly touted as a measles cure
entry.summary: Just because you can grab it off a drugstore shelf doesn’t mean it’s harmless.
